summary:
Italian Fascism, founded by Benito Mussolini in 1919 and brought to power in 1922, was the world's first fascist movement to govern a major state, providing both inspiration and a template for subsequent authoritarian nationalist movements across Europe. Italy had emerged from [[event:110|World War I]] as a victor nation, but one that felt cheated — the "mutilated victory" narrative held that Italy had sacrificed enormously without receiving the territorial rewards promised by the Allies at London in 1915. This grievance combined with economic disruption, fear of Bolshevik-style revolution, and weak parliamentary government to create fertile ground for radical politics.

Mussolini founded the Fasci Italiani di Combattimento in Milan in 1919, recruiting veterans and nationalists with a violent, action-oriented ideology that defied easy left-right categorization. Blackshirt squadrismo — organized violence against socialist and trade union organizations — terrorized opponents across northern Italy with tacit government acquiescence. After the [[event:3331|March on Rome]] in October 1922, King Victor Emmanuel III appointed Mussolini Prime Minister rather than risk civil war. Mussolini consolidated power through legal and extralegal means over the next several years, transforming Italy into a one-party dictatorship by 1928.

The Fascist regime pursued an aggressive foreign policy aimed at establishing a new Roman Empire around the Mediterranean. Italy invaded Ethiopia in 1935–36, annexed Albania in 1939, and formed the Axis alliance with Nazi Germany that drew Italy into [[event:112|World War II]]. Mussolini's domestic record included infrastructure projects, a cult of efficiency, and the Lateran Accords with the Vatican, alongside suppression of opposition, persecution of minorities, and the economic inefficiencies of state corporatism. The regime collapsed in 1943 after Allied invasion of Sicily; Mussolini was deposed, rescued by German forces, and eventually captured and executed by Italian partisans in April 1945.
